reviews.review_description:
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

reviews.review_title: Cool mecha game but lacks substance.
reviews.review_description:
QuantanoID is frankly a good mecha VR game with a limited variation of weapons to choose from, a fast pace nature and good lore. But that is all the game currently has going for it, it lacks the substance and individuality its needs stand out in the sea of other mecha VR games. And here is various points why this is the case;
Fast pace - Even though I said face pace is good for this game, it is way too fast and can lead to you finishing the entire game in less than an hour easily which isn't ideal if you want a memorable experience.
Movement & Controls -  In game you feel way too drifty and lacks proper sustainable control over the mech, that could be just straight jumping or turning and lacks an ability to really change these expect a simple control scheme.
Replayability & Stages - The game lacks any good replayablity and only strives for a better place on the leader board which holds little to none motivation, it doesn't help that every stage is very boring, extremely linear and lacks any personality due to the everything looking the same. There are moment where the rooms open up and add more enemies but nothing to feel like an actually threat.
Story - The story is the only part that adds substance to this game but very minimally, as it lacks the ability to suck the player into the story and created immersion. Even thought there is a prologue of sorts, we are still practically thrown into the story and have no way to feel any connection with our player and any other the other characters in game.
Animations - The animations in game are quite lacking, there are tons of fade to black whenever changing scenes, characters pop in and out with very little effect and enemies have simple or if any animation at all making the game feel blank and lifeless.
Overall I give the game 3 stars because I believe the game can do far better and has the base material to make a very good game if they wished so. And every good game need criticism. (text limit, cant explain more sorry)

This is single handedly one of the best mech games on the quest 2 and 3 it gets everything right about the ui and the movement of said mechs along with the combat.

Also if your a gundam fan then you'll probably like the design and story of the game it feels like it could be a genuine gundam alt time line anime or a standalone 

Lastly this should be the bases for most mecha anime games in the future I'd love to see a gundam game with this style of control and gamplay
